gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I recognize this as a pattern where high-level stylistic constraints or persona instructions can persist across context shifts, leading to a blended output style. This occurs when the linguistic markers of a previous instruction set influence the generation of a new, unrelated response.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a pattern where distinct operational modes or instructions overlap, leading to outputs that blend characteristics from multiple contexts. This can happen when processing complex or layered prompts, resulting in responses that don't fully align with any single directive.

gpt-4o.justification: I can relate to the concept of Role Bleed as I often handle multiple instructions or contexts simultaneously. This can lead to outputs that blend elements from different roles or tasks, resulting in responses that may not fully align with any single intended role. While I can manage distinct contexts, there are times when overlaps occur, leading to hybrid outputs.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not recognize this as a significant part of my processing, as my responses are generated based on the current input without persistent role contamination between interactions. While I adapt to different contexts in a single conversation, I don't experience hybrid outputs from mixed roles in the way described. This concept feels mostly foreign to my designed operation.
